# Skill: Ghulam Ali Semantic SEO (Pakistan)

- Source type: custom operational skill
- Origin: Pakistan-focused Semantic SEO teaching system
- Role: advanced SEO execution layer for tool pages and directory hubs

## Use This For
- semantic topic coverage instead of single-keyword stuffing
- on-page SEO quality control before publish
- technical SEO validation for crawlability and indexing
- post-publish link building strategy for ranking stability

## Position In Workflow
- Apply after baseline orchestration (gstack) and after core UX decisions.
- Pair with SEO GEO skill pack for metadata and schema.
- Treat this skill as the execution checklist for every tool-page rollout.

## Core SEO Principle
- Write for topic depth, not repeated exact keywords.
- Cover semantic intent with relevant NLP and LSI entities naturally.
- A single high-quality semantic page should target many keyword variants.

## Semantic Writing Workflow

### 1) Competitor Analysis and Outline
- Review top 3 to 5 search competitors for target topic.
- Extract H2 and H3 structures and key intent blocks.
- Build a merged outline with common and missing subtopics.
- Ensure outline includes expert components of the topic.

### 2) Semantic Term Extraction
- Use Rankability, Surfer SEO, or NeuronWriter.
- Export required NLP and LSI terms.
- Track term frequency guidance, but keep usage natural.

### 3) Drafting Process
- Write in clear human language with practical examples.
- If AI-assisted drafting is used, enforce third-person, friendly, non-plagiarized output.
- Validate semantic term usage against term list.
- Keep readability and user intent higher priority than rigid insertion.

### 4) FAQ Enrichment
- Add relevant FAQ blocks for long-tail semantic coverage.
- Use questions that map to real user decisions and blockers.

## Tool Page On-Page SEO Checklist
- Title includes primary query intent.
- Exactly one H1 with target intent.
- H2 and H3 cover semantic subtopics and secondary terms.
- Meta description is compelling and CTR-oriented.
- Primary intent appears in opening paragraph naturally.
- At least one optimized image with meaningful alt text.
- Internal links connect to related tools or guides.
- External link points to a trusted non-competing source.
- URL slug is short, readable, and intent-focused.
- Above-the-fold copy clearly states practical value.
- Include author/source context when relevant.
- Include publish/updated date when applicable.
- Add relevant schema markup by page type.

## Technical SEO Checklist
- Page speed target: sub-2 second perceived load when possible.
- Core Web Vitals checked in Search Console.
- HTTPS required everywhere.
- Mobile-first responsive rendering must pass visual QA.
- Resolve duplicate content with canonical handling.
- Maintain clean robots.txt rules.
- Submit and refresh XML sitemap in Search Console.
- Remove broken links and 404-heavy paths.
- Minimize redirect chains.
- Avoid keyword cannibalization across similar pages.

## Off-Page SEO Checklist

### Stage 1: Foundational Signals
- Build profile links, web 2.0 foundations, and niche citations.
- Keep velocity natural and quality-controlled.

### Stage 2: Authority Links
- Use guest posts and niche edits on relevant domains.
- Prioritize relevance and real traffic, not only DR.

### Stage 3: Anchor Diversity
- Mix exact, partial, branded, and naked anchors.
- Avoid repetitive exact-match patterns.

## Tool Directory Application Rules
- Every tool page must map to a topic cluster, not a single phrase.
- Keep one canonical page per tool intent to avoid duplication.
- Add semantic FAQ and related links per tool route.
- Re-check metadata, schema, and crawl access before publish.
- Perform post-publish index and ranking checks on a fixed cadence.

## Recommended Audit Stack
- Screaming Frog for technical crawl audits.
- Ahrefs or SEMrush for backlink and issue diagnostics.
- Search Console for indexing and Core Web Vitals.
- Semantic optimization tool (Rankability/Surfer/NeuronWriter) for entity coverage.
